title: Neolehmannia angustata
taxonomy: Neolehmannia angustata is classified as a species within the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage family. This family comprises flowering plants that are primarily found in temperate and subtropical regions around the world. As a member of the Boraginaceae family, Neolehmannia angustata is an herbaceous perennial plant that produces small, often blue or white flowers. The classification of Neolehmannia angustata is further refined within the genus Neolehmannia, which is thought to be closely related to the genus Eritrichium. The exact classification of Neolehmannia angustata is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new information becomes available.
properties: Neolehmannia angustata is known to possess various therapeutic properties. Studies have shown that it has antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which can help in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit potential in inhibiting the growth of certain microorganisms, making it a promising candidate for the development of new antimicrobial agents. Additionally, Neolehmannia angustata has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory infections. Further research is necessary to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Neolehmannia angustata and to explore its potential applications in modern medicine.

intro: Neolehmannia angustata, commonly known as Yellow Bird's Eye or Neolehmannia, is a plant species in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Neolehmannia angustata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Neolehmannia is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
